pattern: Padding all three numbers to four digits turns the digit-by-digit minimum into a plain positional comparison, and parsing the result strips any leading zeros for free.
hints: Pad all three numbers to exactly four digits with leading zeros before comparing anything.
Walk the four digit positions and take the minimum of the three digits at each position.
Join the four resulting digits into a string and parse it back to an integer, which naturally drops any leading zeros.

edge_inputs.description: padding creates leading zeros that are then dropped
no padding needed, straightforward digit minimum
all three numbers at the minimum
all three numbers at the maximum
the key itself is entirely zero
one number is far smaller and dominates every digit position

stress.description: random triples across the full range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    return [rng.randint(1, 9999), rng.randint(1, 9999), rng.randint(1, 9999)]
stress.note: single random triple per generated case

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def generateKey(self, num1, num2, num3):
        # Independent implementation using arithmetic digit extraction instead
        # of string padding.
        digits = []
        for i in range(4):
            div = 10 ** i
            d1 = (num1 // div) % 10
            d2 = (num2 // div) % 10
            d3 = (num3 // div) % 10
            digits.append(min(d1, d2, d3))
        value = 0
        for i in range(3, -1, -1):
            value = value * 10 + digits[i]
        return value
